When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the L-shape changed 20 cells and gameplay changed. Looking at the image, the purple block (p) in the upper frame now has a white border around it, suggesting it's selected. The L-shape click seems to have selected the adjacent block.
Let me check what exactly changed by comparing before and after.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed when clicking the L-shape area
lines_before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
lines_after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("Differences:")
for i in range(len(lines_before)):
    if lines_before[i] != lines_after[i]:
        print(f"Row {i}:")
        print(f"  Before: {lines_before[i]}")
        print(f"  After:  {lines_after[i]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Clicking on the L-shape area (row 22, col 30) actually selected the purple block (p) by adding a white border around it. The white 'W' characters form a border around the purple block.
So clicking near a block selects it (adds a white border). This is different from what I thought.
Now I need to understand what happens when a block is selected. Maybe I can move it, swap it, or delete it.
Let me think about what actions I can take with a selected block:
1. Click on another block to swap them?
2. Press SPACE to do something with the selected block?
3. Click on an empty slot to move the selected block there?
Let me try clicking on the N block in the upper-right slot while the purple block is selected, to see if they swap.
Actually wait, I should first understand the selection mechanism better. Let me check if clicking on a block directly selects it, or if clicking near it selects it.
Actually, I realize I've been wasting too many actions. Let me think about what the most efficient path forward is.
The current state has the purple block selected (white border). Let me try clicking on the N block in the upper-right to see if they swap.

<parameter=code>
# Purple block is selected (white border). Let me try clicking on the N block to swap them.
# N block is at approximately row 21-24, col 36-39
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 22, 'col': 37}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
